title: fix: tests webserver syntaxwarning
body:
## Title
fix: tests webserver syntaxwarning

## Description
> Changed in Python version 3.12: Unrecognized escape sequences produce a SyntaxWarning. In a future Python version they will be eventually a SyntaxError.
[2.4.1.1. Escape sequences](https://docs.python.org/3/reference/lexical_analysis.html#index-23)

Change to raw string literal to fix:
```
/webserver.py:17: SyntaxWarning: invalid escape sequence '\d'
  elif re.match("/status/(\d+)", self.path):
/webserver.py:18: SyntaxWarning: invalid escape sequence '\d'
  result = re.match("/status/(\d+)", self.path)
```

step_plan.summary: Fix Python 3.12 SyntaxWarning by converting regex patterns in the test webserver to raw strings to properly handle escape sequences.

steps.operation: Convert problematic regex literals to raw strings in Handler.do_GET
steps.reason: Python 3.12 raises SyntaxWarning for invalid escape sequences in non-raw strings. Converting the regex literals to raw strings preserves existing behavior while silencing the warning (e.g., change "/status/(\d+)" to r"/status/(\d+)"). The change is isolated to the test webserver and does not alter runtime logic.
steps.side_effects: No functional changes; tests should continue to pass. May require re-running the test suite to confirm warning is gone; negligible impact on other modules and API behavior.
